chief_complaint: Three patients, aged 45, 58, and 72, presented with massive gastrointestinal bleeding, manifested as recurrent hematemesis and hematochezia with large-volume bleeding.
history: Dieulafoy's disease is a rare condition that can cause acute, life-threatening massive gastrointestinal bleeding and is prone to misdiagnosis and missed diagnosis. The patients were admitted to the hospital with a history of massive gastrointestinal bleeding.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia, hypotension. Physical examination: abdominal tenderness, guarding.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: anemia, coagulopathy. Imaging: upper GI series showing diverticula of the descending duodenum. Endoscopy: active bleeding located in the descending duodenum.
correct_diagnosis: Dieulafoy's disease within diverticula of the descending duodenum
differentials: Dieulafoy's disease within diverticula of the descending duodenum
correct_ranking: Dieulafoy's disease within diverticula of the descending duodenum
